items.explain: Section 13.4 defines FAIR as Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.

items.prompt: Accessible under FAIR always requires unrestricted public download with no access controls.
items.explain: Accessible means clear standardized terms; Zenodo and Figshare may apply privacy or ethical restrictions.

items.prompt: Indexing a cancer genomics dataset in NCBI or ENA primarily supports which FAIR letter?
items.explain: Example 13.12 uses public-archive indexing so researchers can locate the dataset.

items.prompt: Name one standard genomics file type or controlled vocabulary used for interoperability in this part.
items.rubric: Accept FASTQ, VCF, Gene Ontology / GO, UMLS, or equivalent from Example 13.14.

items.prompt: Preferring community formats such as CSV, JSON, HDF5, or NetCDF supports the Reusable principle.
items.explain: Example 13.15 recommends community formats because they are widely supported by analysis tools.
